definition: One if the assigned source choice is an independent channel before evidence is observed.
One if the submitted report equals the participant's observed synthetic signal.
One if the action equals the treatment-specific recommendation.
Distinct evidence-supported actions divided by the team action budget.
One if at least one team action covers the realized synthetic target.
Team discovery value minus declared source and mechanism costs.
Number of submitted treatment-permitted messages.
Truthful disclosed reports divided by all disclosed reports.
Sum of squared action shares within a team-round.
Sum of squared source-choice shares within a team-round.
Elapsed synthetic decision time from screen display to submission.
Pre-specified confidence response submitted before outcome feedback.
Declared base reward plus transfer minus evidence cost.
Total externally financed transfer in a team-round.
One if behavior lies in the treatment's frozen exact-model response set.
Actions using the public clue divided by all submitted actions.
One when exactly one role conditions its action on the public clue.
One when discovery is made by an action differing from the public clue.
Actions choosing the registered third option on clue disagreement.
Candidates receiving at least the registered threshold number of actions.
Realized viable-team discovery divided by the frozen planner value.
Submitted actions before the fixed horizon or registered stopping time.
Fixed action budget minus submitted actions at the registered stopping time.
